discovered_by: claude-opus-4-7 during §6.H spot-check before storing operator-provided test credentials in .env
summary: RuTracker test credentials nobody85perfect/ironman1985 were already present in 5 tracked files when operator (re-)provided them today for use in automated testing. The credentials had landed in git history at earlier points in the SP-3a + early-distribute cycles (Lava-Android-1.2.0-1020). §6.H clause 4 ("No credential strings in source code") was historically violated; pre-push hook + check-constitution.sh credential-pattern grep did not catch literal username/password strings — only known SHA patterns of structural credential markers.
remediation_landed_this_cycle: Redacted all 5 tracked files in-place: literal 'nobody85perfect' and 'ironman1985' replaced with '<redacted-per-§6.H>' placeholders.
Pushed redaction commit to both §6.W mirrors (github + gitlab) — closes the LIVE leak surface for new clones.

remediation_owed_post_cycle.action: Rotate the RuTracker account password (treat the historical leak as a compromise).
remediation_owed_post_cycle.blocks: Per §6.H clause 6: credentials MUST be rotated after accidental commit. Until rotated, the leaked credentials remain valid at rutracker.org for anyone who pulled an older commit.

remediation_owed_post_cycle.action: Extend scripts/check-constitution.sh credential-pattern grep to catch additional literal patterns: a) any string matching common username + length-1+ password pair within the same file, b) the specific operator-provided strings (after rotation, as a regression guard for that pair specifically).
remediation_owed_post_cycle.blocks: Without strengthened grep, the next set of operator-provided credentials could leak again the same way.

remediation_owed_post_cycle.action: git-history purge of the leaked credentials via git-filter-repo (or BFG Repo-Cleaner) + force-push to all §6.W mirrors. This is §6.T.3 territory (no force-push without explicit operator authorization) — requires per-operation operator approval.
remediation_owed_post_cycle.blocks: Without history purge, anyone who clones the repo can still see the historical credentials in older commits even after the redaction commit lands.

rule_violated: §6.H clauses 4 + 5 + 6 (No credential strings in source code; Pre-push credential scan; Leak response protocol)
scope: All 5 files were authored before §6.H's pre-push hook integration was complete. The hook today catches NEW introductions but did not flag PRE-EXISTING tracked content during its own activation. This incident is the residual from that gap.
evidence_of_redaction: git log -1 --name-only on this commit shows all 5 files modified; `git ls-files | xargs grep -lE 'nobody85perfect|ironman1985'` returns empty post-redaction.
constitutional_classification: project-specific (the leak instances are Lava-specific test credentials; the §6.H rule itself is universal per HelixConstitution-derived inheritance).
